task.title: Setting preferences for features used by New COBOL Program wizard

titlealts.searchtitle: Setting preferences for features that are used by New COBOL Program wizard

task.shortdesc: You can define and customize the features available to your generated COBOL programs. Some predefined features are provided.

keywords.indexterm: setting preferences for features used by New COBOL Program wizard

context.p:
You can do these tasks:
List current features for COBOL generation and add new features
Resequence, rename, exclude from the COBOL Program wizard, or delete some or all of the features
Export features (including ones that you created) so that they can be shared
Import features
Restore all features to their product defaults

steps.stepsection: To work with preferences for COBOL generation features, complete the following steps:

step.cmd: On the Preferences page, navigate to COBOL Features .
step.stepresult: A list of the currently configured COBOL features is displayed. The default features are as follows: Use BMS Maps Invoke CICS commands Use SQL statements Handle SQL error return codes

step.cmd: Perform one or more of the following tasks:

chrow.choption: Change the order of the list

ol.li: Select the name of a feature.

chrow.choption: Add a feature to the list

ol.li: Click New. The New feature window opens.
Type the name of the new feature and click OK.

For each feature, you can define and customize the code that you want to insert into each section of your COBOL program (for example, the Working Storage section or the Procedure Division). In the tabbed editors for each section, enter the code to define each feature. The tabs are as follows:
Environment
File
Working Storage
Local Storage
Linkage
Procedure
Select the name of a feature, and click Rename. The Rename feature window opens.
Type the new name, and click OK.
Clear the checkbox. You do not need to delete the feature.
Select the name of a feature, and click Delete. The Confirm delete window opens.

chrow.choption: Save one or more features to a file

ol.li: Click Export All, or select one or more features and click Export. The Export COBOL features window opens.
Specify the location and file name of the XML file to be created, and click Save.

chrow.choption: Use features from an existing XML file

ol.li: Click Import. The Import COBOL features window opens.
Select an XML file, and click Open.
Click Restore Defaults. The Confirm Restore Defaults window opens.
Click OK. All COBOL feature customizations are removed. The product defaults are restored.
